bio.quote: Hildvarð Bruneson is the Djúpdvergr Forgemaster of Djúphól — a 140-year-old craftsman who has maintained his forge and his people through the Fimbulwinter through sheer stubbornness and skill. He is d

system.description:
Dwarves

Stereotype: Greedy as a dragon, stubborn as a glacier.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Dwarves

Stereotype: Greedy as a dragon, stubborn as a glacier.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: You can paint a rune onto your skin (10 min work). Only one active at a time. * *Detection:* Glows near chosen creature/object type (20 sq). * *Light:* Sheds light (10 sq). Toggle as free action. * *Voice:* Rune-echo bond with one willing creature (1 mile; functions as a telepathic bond).

novice.description: You can paint a rune onto your skin (10 min work). Only one active at a time. * *Detection:* Glows near chosen creature/object type (20 sq). * *Light:* Sheds light (10 sq). Toggle as free action. * *Voice:* Rune-echo bond with one willing creature (1 mile; functions as a telepathic bond).

system.description: You cannot be made *weakened*.

novice.description: You cannot be made *weakened*.

system.description: Reshape unworked stone with song (1 hour ritual).

novice.description: Reshape unworked stone with song (1 hour ritual).

system.description: +1 Resistance.

novice.description: +1 Resistance.

system.description:
A bog-iron and peat community built on stilts above hungry ground, with seasonal movement between safe platforms and hard-learned routes.

Language: Myrmål

Environment: Rural; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Labor

novice.description:
A bog-iron and peat community built on stilts above hungry ground, with seasonal movement between safe platforms and hard-learned routes.

Language: Myrmål

Environment: Rural; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Labor

system.description:
Skills: Crafting, plus two more skills from the crafting or lore skill groups (Quick Build: Crafting, Cunning, Blacksmithing).

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

Perk: *Workman’s Shortcut*. Once per extended rest, reduce the time of one mundane crafting or repair project by half.

novice.description:
Skills: Crafting, plus two more skills from the crafting or lore skill groups (Quick Build: Crafting, Cunning, Blacksmithing).

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

Perk: *Workman’s Shortcut*. Once per extended rest, reduce the time of one mundane crafting or repair project by half.

system.description: Whenever you make a test to craft something and don’t have a skill that applies to the test, you gain a +1 bonus to the attribute test.

novice.description: Whenever you make a test to craft something and don’t have a skill that applies to the test, you gain a +1 bonus to the attribute test.

system.description: Air for movement. Earth for permanence. Fire for destruction. Water for change. Green for growth. Rot for death. Void for the mystery— that which cannot be known. Years of study and practice and poring over tomes brought you the revelations that allow you to manipulate these building blocks of reality. Now you use your mastery of the seven elements to destroy, create, and warp the world with magic. As an elementalist, you can unleash your wrath across a field of foes, debilitate enemies, ward yourself and allies, manipulate terrain, warp space, and more. Your elemental specialization determines which of these talents you excel at.

novice.description: Air for movement. Earth for permanence. Fire for destruction. Water for change. Green for growth. Rot for death. Void for the mystery— that which cannot be known. Years of study and practice and poring over tomes brought you the revelations that allow you to manipulate these building blocks of reality. Now you use your mastery of the seven elements to destroy, create, and warp the world with magic. As an elementalist, you can unleash your wrath across a field of foes, debilitate enemies, ward yourself and allies, manipulate terrain, warp space, and more. Your elemental specialization determines which of these talents you excel at.

system.description: Void is the element of the mystery. Void abilities warp space and reality, allowing you to teleport, create illusions, and make things incorporeal.

novice.description: Void is the element of the mystery. Void abilities warp space and reality, allowing you to teleport, create illusions, and make things incorporeal.

system.description: You use your immersion in the mystery of void magic to expand the reach of that magic better than other mages. The distance of all your abilities that have the Magic, Ranged, and Void keywords increases by 2 meters.

novice.description: You use your immersion in the mystery of void magic to expand the reach of that magic better than other mages. The distance of all your abilities that have the Magic, Ranged, and Void keywords increases by 2 meters.

system.description:
You instantly recognize illusions for what they are, you can see invisible creatures, and supernatural effects can’t conceal creatures and objects from you. Additionally, you always know if an area or object you observe is magical or affected by magic, and you know the specifics of what that magic can do.

You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.LgMzJ9xHxs6ggP1y]{Shared Void Sense} ability.

novice.description:
You instantly recognize illusions for what they are, you can see invisible creatures, and supernatural effects can’t conceal creatures and objects from you. Additionally, you always know if an area or object you observe is magical or affected by magic, and you know the specifics of what that magic can do.

You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.LgMzJ9xHxs6ggP1y]{Shared Void Sense} ability.

system.description: Each time you finish a extended rest, you can open a two-way portal that leads to any place you have previously been. You and your allies can pass through the portal, which remains open for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a main action.

novice.description: Each time you finish a extended rest, you can open a two-way portal that leads to any place you have previously been. You and your allies can pass through the portal, which remains open for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a main action.

system.description: While you have 3 or more essence and are not dying, you exude an aura of magic whose distance is equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. The effects within the area of the aura are based on your specialization, as shown on the Mantle of Essence Specialization Effects table. You can activate and deactivate the aura at will (no action required).

novice.description: While you have 3 or more essence and are not dying, you exude an aura of magic whose distance is equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. The effects within the area of the aura are based on your specialization, as shown on the Mantle of Essence Specialization Effects table. You can activate and deactivate the aura at will (no action required).

system.description: The area provides concealment for you and your allies.

novice.description: The area provides concealment for you and your allies.

system.description:
Solidity is merely a suggestion to you. Mundane barriers that are 1 meters (2 m) thick or less do not block your senses or line of effect. You can only sense or have line of effect past one such barrier at a time.

Additionally, whenever you use a void ability, you or one ally within distance of the ability can teleport a number of meters equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.

novice.description:
Solidity is merely a suggestion to you. Mundane barriers that are 1 meters (2 m) thick or less do not block your senses or line of effect. You can only sense or have line of effect past one such barrier at a time.

Additionally, whenever you use a void ability, you or one ally within distance of the ability can teleport a number of meters equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.

system.description: A protective field of void magic absorbs violence aimed at you, then lets you hurl it back at your enemies. The first time each round that you take damage, you .

novice.description: A protective field of void magic absorbs violence aimed at you, then lets you hurl it back at your enemies. The first time each round that you take damage, you .

system.description:
You can wear light armor and wield light weapons effectively, even though you don’t have a kit. While you wear light armor, you gain a +3 bonus to Toughness, and that bonus increases by 3 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. While you wield a light weapon, you gain a +1 damage bonus with weapon abilities, including free strikes. You can use light armor treasures and light weapon treasures.

If you have a kit, you can’t take this enchantment.

novice.description:
You can wear light armor and wield light weapons effectively, even though you don’t have a kit. While you wear light armor, you gain a +3 bonus to Toughness, and that bonus increases by 3 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. While you wield a light weapon, you gain a +1 damage bonus with weapon abilities, including free strikes. You can use light armor treasures and light weapon treasures.

If you have a kit, you can’t take this enchantment.

system.description:
You channel the substance of creation in the form of a Heroic Resource called essence, gathering and burning it to cast and maintain spells.

Essence in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain essence equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 essence.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 meters takes damage that isn’t untyped or holy damage, you {gain 1 essence}.

You lose any remaining essence at the end of the encounter.

Essence Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain essence outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost essence without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs essence, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use a persistent ability outside of combat (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.qOIxRq9d3diTKJzO]{Persistent Magic}), you can maintain it for a number of rounds equal to your Milestones.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited essence on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of essence equal to your Milestones.

novice.description:
You channel the substance of creation in the form of a Heroic Resource called essence, gathering and burning it to cast and maintain spells.

Essence in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain essence equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 essence.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 meters takes damage that isn’t untyped or holy damage, you {gain 1 essence}.

You lose any remaining essence at the end of the encounter.

Essence Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain essence outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost essence without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs essence, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use a persistent ability outside of combat (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.qOIxRq9d3diTKJzO]{Persistent Magic}), you can maintain it for a number of rounds equal to your Milestones.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited essence on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of essence equal to your Milestones.

system.description:
Some of your heroic abilities have a persistent effect entry. For example, the Instantaneous Excavation ability has an effect noted as “Persistent 1.” Whenever you use a persistent ability, you decide whether you want to maintain it, and start doing so immediately after you first use the ability. If you maintain a persistent ability in combat, you reduce the amount of essence you earn at the start of your turn by an amount equal to the ability’s persistent value, which enables the ability’s persistent effect. All your active persistent abilities end at the end of the encounter.

You can’t maintain any abilities that would make you earn a negative amount of essence at the start of your turn. You can stop maintaining an ability at any time (no action required).

If you maintain the same ability on several targets and the effect includes a attribute test, you make that roll once and apply the same effect to all targets. A creature can’t be affected by multiple instances of a persistent ability.

If you take damage equal to or greater than [[lookup 5*@R evaluate=true]]{5 times your Reason score} in one turn, you stop maintaining any persistent abilities. For instance, if you have a Reason score of 2 and are maintaining Instantaneous Excavation, taking 10 or more damage in one turn causes you to stop maintaining the ability.

All your active persistent abilities end at the end of the encounter.

If you maintain the same ability on several targets and the effect includes a attribute test, you make that roll once and apply the same effect to all targets. A creature can’t be affected by multiple instances of a persistent ability.

For instance, if you have a Reason score of 2 and are maintaining Instantaneous Excavation, taking 10 or more damage in one turn causes you to stop maintaining the ability.

system.description: The first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 meters takes damage that isn’t untyped or holy damage, you gain 2 essence instead of 1.

novice.description: The first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 meters takes damage that isn’t untyped or holy damage, you gain 2 essence instead of 1.

system.description: Without needing to make a test—and even without tools—you can quickly jury-rig or repair a mundane item or piece of equipment related to a skill you have from the crafting skill group. That item lasts for 1 hour or works for one use or activation (whichever comes first, as the Director determines), then breaks beyond repair. For example, if you have the Carpentry skill, you could repair a rickety wooden bridge long enough for a group of creatures to cross it, or build a simple shovel made of wood that can be used for 1 hour.

novice.description: Without needing to make a test—and even without tools—you can quickly jury-rig or repair a mundane item or piece of equipment related to a skill you have from the crafting skill group. That item lasts for 1 hour or works for one use or activation (whichever comes first, as the Director determines), then breaks beyond repair. For example, if you have the Carpentry skill, you could repair a rickety wooden bridge long enough for a group of creatures to cross it, or build a simple shovel made of wood that can be used for 1 hour.

system.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

novice.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.
